Title: The draft genome and transcriptome of Cannabis sativa

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal and intoxicating bioactive compound production
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flower tissues; elevated in flowering stages; increased transcript abundance in Purple Kush relative to Finola
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in pre-flower, early flower, and mid-flower tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes cannabinoid biosynthesis by supplying hexanoyl-CoA precursor
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAE1 is considered the more likely candidate for hexanoyl-CoA synthetase involved in cannabinoid biosynthesis. 2. Its transcript is highly expressed in flower tissues where cannabinoids accumulate. 3. AAE1 transcript abundance is increased in Purple Kush, consistent with elevated cannabinoid production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq expression profiling across tissues and between chemotypes; genome assembly analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing (PK: SRA SRP008673; Finola: SRP008728); RNA-seq (PK: SRP008726; Finola: SRP008729)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to enhance precursor supply for cannabinoid engineering, especially in flower/trichome-active strategies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states AAE1 is a more likely candidate than AAE3 for the hexanoyl-CoA synthetase step, but this is partly based on unpublished results; causal enzymatic proof is not provided. PK genome contains two copies on scaffold1750 (JH227821) and scaffold29030 (JH245535).

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal and intoxicating bioactive compound production
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not highlighted as flower-enriched relative to AAE1; copy number expanded in Purple Kush
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Possible but unproven contribution to cannabinoid biosynthesis; copy-number expansion in PK does not establish causality
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAE3 shows much expanded genomic coverage in Purple Kush relative to Finola. 2. It is similar to Arabidopsis At4g05160, which can activate medium- and long-chain fatty acids including hexanoate. 3. The expansion is interpreted as likely due to processed pseudogenes, and its role in cannabinoid biosynthesis remains uncertain.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomic read-depth/copy-number analysis; homology inference
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing/read-depth analysis (PK SRP008673; Finola SRP008728)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low immediate value until functional role is validated; avoid assuming copy-number expansion is beneficial
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Copy expansion likely reflects pseudogene proliferation rather than functional gain
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors explicitly state AAE3 encodes an enzyme of unknown function in PK and may represent processed pseudogenes.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Published gene: AB057805; PK transcript: PK29242.1/JP450547; PK genomic scaffold19603/JH239911
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidocyclizes cannabigerolic acid to form Δ9-tetrahydrocannabinolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: psychoactive cannabinoid precursor underlying marijuana potency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in PK flowers at all stages; absent from Finola transcriptome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in pre-flower, early flower, and mid-flower; exclusive occurrence in PK transcriptome among compared chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes THCA production and distinguishes marijuana-type chemotype from hemp
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THCAS is present as a functional gene in the PK genome and transcriptome and is highly expressed in female flowers. 2. THCAS is absent as a functional transcript in Finola, explaining lack of THC production in hemp. 3. PK also contains a THCAS-like pseudogene, while Finola likely carries pseudogenic THCAS reads.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome assembly; transcriptome assembly; RNA-seq expression profiling across tissues and chemotypes; sequence comparison to published THCAS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary marker and engineering target for THCA-dominant chemotypes; useful for breeding high-THC or THC-free cultivars depending on selection/editing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing THCAS may increase psychoactivity and regulatory burden
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A THCAS-like pseudogene on scaffold1330 (JH227480) was identified in PK. The study supports selection for THCAS over CBDAS as a major domestication event.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidocyclizes cannabigerolic acid to form cannabidiolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dominant functional synthase in Finola flowers; absent as functional transcript in PK
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in Finola hemp flowers that contain moderate CBDA; only pseudogenic remnants detected in PK
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CBDA accumulation and underlies hemp-type chemotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBDAS replaces THCAS as the functional terminal cannabinoid synthase in Finola. 2. PK contains multiple CBDAS pseudogenes with stop codons/frameshifts and only a short likely decay-derived transcript fragment. 3. Competition between CBDAS and THCAS for CBGA may shape THCA:CBDA ratios.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genome/transcriptome sequence analysis; RNA-seq expression comparison; pseudogene identification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing and RNA-seq (Finola SRP008728, SRP008729; PK SRP008673, SRP008726)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key marker/target for breeding CBD-rich hemp and reducing THC through pathway competition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Selection against CBDAS may increase THC; preserving CBDAS is important for low-THC chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In PK, three CBDAS pseudogene-containing scaffolds were identified, accounting for genomic reads without functional enzyme production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses hexanoyl-CoA in the polyketide pathway leading to olivetolic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowering stages; increased in PK relative to Finola
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Flower-preferential, consistent with trichome cannabinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor formation for cannabinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OLS is part of the core cannabinoid pathway and is strongly expressed in flower tissues. 2. OLS expression is higher in PK than in Finola, consistent with elevated cannabinoid production. 3. OLS is duplicated in the PK genome.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq tissue profiling; chemotype comparison; genome assembly/copy analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome and RNA-seq datasets from PK and Finola
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing cannabinoid precursor supply, potentially in combination with AAE1, PT and terminal synthases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PK has duplicated OLS copies on scaffold15717 (JH226441) and scaffold16618 (JH237993).

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Prenylation step linking polyketide and isoprenoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates olivetolic acid with geranyl diphosphate to generate cannabigerolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in flowering tissues and elevated in PK relative to Finola as part of the cannabinoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CBGA formation, supplying substrate for THCAS/CBDAS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PT is a core cannabinoid pathway enzyme connecting precursor pathways to CBGA production. 2. PT expression is highest in flowering tissues. 3. PT is more strongly expressed in PK than in Finola, paralleling increased cannabinoid output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway annotation with RNA-seq expression profiling across tissues and chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering node for increasing CBGA flux toward major cannabinoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol not further specified in this paper beyond PT.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway supplying cannabinoid isoprenoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclodiphosphate formation in the plastidial MEP pathway supplying GPP precursor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MEP pathway genes are mostly highest in flowering stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports cannabinoid biosynthesis by supplying isoprenoid precursor flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MDS is part of the precursor MEP pathway associated with cannabinoid biosynthesis. 2. Its transcript required manual merging during assembly, indicating the transcriptome captured this pathway gene. 3. MEP pathway gene expression is elevated in flower tissues where cannabinoids are synthesized.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome assembly/manual curation; RNA-seq tissue profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DXS; DXR; MCT; CMK; HDS; HDR; GPP synthase lsu; GPP synthase ssu
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply engineering target when stacked with downstream cannabinoid enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is specifically discussed in Results as one of the analyzed pathway genes and manually resolved in the assembly.
